simple_types.name: Call_Modes
simple_types.code:
type Call_Modes is
   (Simple_Call, Conditional_Call, Asynchronous_Call, Timed_Call);

simple_types.name: Entry_Index
simple_types.code: type Entry_Index is range Interrupt_Entry .. Max_Entry;

documentation.description: ID of an entry contained within a protected object or task

simple_types.name: Task_Entry_Index
simple_types.code:
type Task_Entry_Index is new Entry_Index
  range Null_Task_Entry .. Max_Task_Entry;

documentation.description:
ID of an entry contained in a task.

Note: while we do not support task entries on this runtime, we defined
Task_Entry_Index so the Ada_Task_Control_Block type preserves its
Entry_Num discriminate for capability with other runtimes (so the
expander does not have to accommodate different versions)

simple_types.name: Task_States
simple_types.code:
type Task_States is
  (Unactivated,
   Runnable,
   Terminated,
   Activator_Sleep,
   Acceptor_Sleep,
   Entry_Caller_Sleep,
   Async_Select_Sleep,
   Delay_Sleep,
,
,
,
   …,
   Interrupt_Server_Blocked_On_Event_Flag
  );

enumeration_literals.name: Unactivated
enumeration_literals.description:
Task was created but has not been activated. It cannot be executing
For all states from here down, the task has been activated. In
addition, for all states from here down, except for Terminated,
the task may be executing.

enumeration_literals.name: Runnable
enumeration_literals.description:
Task is not blocked for any reason known to Ada. (It may be waiting
for a mutex, though.) It is conceptually "executing" in normal mode.

enumeration_literals.name: Terminated
enumeration_literals.description: The task is terminated, in the sense of ARM 9.3 (5)

enumeration_literals.name: Activator_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Task is waiting for created tasks to complete activation

enumeration_literals.name: Acceptor_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Task is waiting on an accept or selective wait statement

enumeration_literals.name: Entry_Caller_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Task is waiting on an entry call

enumeration_literals.name: Async_Select_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description:
Task is waiting to start the abortable part of an asynchronous select
statement.

enumeration_literals.name: Delay_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Task is waiting on a delay statement

enumeration_literals.name: Master_Completion_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description:
Master completion has two phases. In Phase 1 the task is sleeping
in Complete_Master having completed a master within itself, and is
waiting for the tasks dependent on that master to become terminated
or waiting on a terminate Phase.

enumeration_literals.name: Master_Phase_2_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description:
In Phase 2 the task is sleeping in Complete_Master waiting for tasks
on terminate alternatives to finish terminating.

enumeration_literals.name: Interrupt_Server_Idle_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Special uses of sleep, for server tasks within the run-time system

enumeration_literals.name: Interrupt_Server_Blocked_Interrupt_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Special uses of sleep, for server tasks within the run-time system

enumeration_literals.name: Timer_Server_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Special uses of sleep, for server tasks within the run-time system

enumeration_literals.name: AST_Server_Sleep
enumeration_literals.description: Special uses of sleep, for server tasks within the run-time system

enumeration_literals.name: Asynchronous_Hold
enumeration_literals.description: The task has been held by Asynchronous_Task_Control.Hold_Task

array_types.name: Task_List
array_types.code: type Task_List is array (Positive range <>) of Task_Id;

record_types.name: Ada_Task_Control_Block

fields.name: Entry_Num
fields.description:
The discriminant Entry_Num is not needed, but we keep it here for
compatibility reasons with the rest of the run times, so that the
expander does not need to know which run time is being used.

fields.name: Entry_Call
fields.description:
Protection: This field is used on entry call queues associated with
protected objects, and is protected by the protected object lock.

record_types.name: Common_ATCB

fields.name: State
fields.description:
Encodes some basic information about the state of a task, including
whether it has been activated, whether it is sleeping, and whether
it is terminated.

Protection: Only accessed by Self

fields.name: Base_CPU
fields.description: Protection: Only written during initialization, accessed by anyone

fields.name: Base_Priority
fields.description:
Base priority

Protection: Only written by Self, accessed by anyone

fields.name: Protected_Action_Nesting
fields.description:
The dynamic level of protected action nesting for this task. This
field is needed for checking whether potentially blocking operations
are invoked from protected actions. pragma Atomic is used because it
can be read/written from protected interrupt handlers.

fields.name: LL
fields.description:
Control block used by underlying low-level tasking service (GNULLI)

Protection: This is used only by the GNULLI implementation, which
takes care of all of its synchronization.

fields.name: Wakeup_Signaled
fields.description:
Variable which reflects whether another thread has performed a
Wakeup operation on the thread. It may happen when a task is about
to suspend itself, but it is preempted just before by the task that
is going to awake it. Used by POSIX-based RTOSes.

Protection: Common.LL.L.

fields.name: Task_Arg
fields.description:
The argument to task procedure. Currently unused, this will provide
a handle for discriminant information.

Protection: Part of the synchronization between Self and Activator.
Activator writes it, once, before Self starts executing. Thereafter,
Self only reads it.

fields.name: Task_Entry_Point
fields.description:
Information needed to call the procedure containing the code for
the body of this task.

Protection: Part of the synchronization between Self and Activator.
Activator writes it, once, before Self starts executing. Self reads
it, once, as part of its execution.

fields.name: Compiler_Data
fields.description:
Task-specific data needed by compiler to store per-task structures

Protection: Only accessed by Self

fields.name: Activation_Link
fields.description:
Used to link this task to a list of tasks to be activated

Protection: Only used by Activator. Once the task is activated, this
can also be reused by System.Tasking.Debug for the list of known
tasks.

fields.name: Task_Info
fields.description:
System-specific attributes of the task as specified by the
Task_Info pragma.

record_types.name: Entry_Call_Record
Entry_Queue
Stack_Info
TSD

fields.name: Pri_Stack_Info
fields.description: Stack address and size of the task

fields.name: Sec_Stack_Ptr
fields.description: Pointer of currently allocated secondary stack

access_types.name: Access_Boolean
access_types.code: type Access_Boolean is access all Boolean;

access_types.name: Activation_Chain_Access
access_types.code: type Activation_Chain_Access is access all Activation_Chain;

access_types.name: Entry_Call_Link
access_types.code: type Entry_Call_Link is access all Entry_Call_Record;

access_types.name: Task_Id
access_types.code: type Task_Id is access all Ada_Task_Control_Block;

access_types.name: Task_Procedure_Access
access_types.code: type Task_Procedure_Access is access procedure (Arg : System.Address);

access_types.name: Termination_Handler
access_types.code: type Termination_Handler is access protected procedure (T : Task_Id);

documentation.description: Represent a protected procedure to be executed when a task terminates

subtypes.name: Extended_Priority
subtypes.code:
subtype Extended_Priority is
  Integer range Idle_Priority .. Any_Priority'Last;

documentation.description: Priority range that also includes the idle priority
Entry index for a canceled entry
A priority lower than any user priority. Used by the idle task
Entry index for interrupt entry
Maximum entry index value
Maximum entry index for a task
Entry index indicating no entry
Entry index for no task entry
No affinity specified
No specified priority. This value is also hard-coded in gnatbind.

This is the fall-back handler that applies to all the tasks in the
partition (this is only for Ravenscar-compliant systems).

Return the address of the task specific secondary stack, as expected by
System.Secondary_Stack.

This procedure constitutes the first part of the initialization of the
GNARL. This includes creating data structures to make the initial thread
into the environment task. The last part of the initialization is done
in System.Tasking.Initialization or System.Tasking.Restricted.Stages.
All the initializations used to be in Tasking.Initialization, but this
is no longer possible with the run time simplification (including
optimized PO and the restricted run time) since one cannot rely on
System.Tasking.Initialization being present, as was done before.

subprograms.name: Initialize_ATCB

documentation.description:
Initialize fields of a TCB and link into global TCB structures
Call this only with abort deferred and holding All_Tasks_L.

This is the compiler interface version of this function. Do not call
from the run-time system.

Retrieve from the TCB of the task the allocated size of its stack,
either the system default or the size specified by a pragma. This
is in general a non-static value that can depend on discriminants
of the task.

generic_instantiations.name: To_Address
To_Task_Id
